content.problemStatement:
In the nation of Chefland there are villages at co-ordinates (x,y)(x,y)(x,y) whenever xxx and yyy are non-zero odd co-prime integers. xxx can be either positive or negative, but yyy is always positive.

In each village (x,y)(x,y)(x,y) there is a crossroads, with the roads marked North, South, East, West.

Road East leads to (x+2y,y)(x+2y, y)(x+2y,y).

Road West leads to (x‚àí2y,y)(x-2y, y)(x‚àí2y,y).

Road North leads to either (x,y+2x)(x, y+2x)(x,y+2x) or (‚àíx,‚àí(y+2x))(-x, -(y+2x))(‚àíx,‚àí(y+2x)),  chosen so that the new yyy co-ordinate is positive.

Road South leads to either (x,y‚àí2x)(x, y-2x)(x,y‚àí2x) or (‚àíx,‚àí(y‚àí2x))(-x, -(y-2x))(‚àíx,‚àí(y‚àí2x)), chosen so that the new yyy co-ordinate is positive.

Some citizens of Chefland prefer these equivalent definitions for the North and South roads:

Road North leads to {(x,y+2x)when y+2x>0(‚àíx,‚àí(y+2x))when y+2x<0\left\{ \begin{array} {ll} (x,y+2x) & \quad {\tt when}\ y+2x > 0 \\ (-x,-(y+2x)) & \quad {\tt when}\  y+2x < 0\end{array} \right.{(x,y+2x)(‚àíx,‚àí(y+2x))‚Äãwhen y+2x>0when y+2x<0‚Äã

Road South leads to {(x,y‚àí2x)when y‚àí2x>0(‚àíx,‚àí(y‚àí2x))when y‚àí2x<0\left\{ \begin{array} {ll} (x,y-2x) & \quad {\tt when}\ y-2x > 0 \\ (-x,-(y-2x)) & \quad {\tt when}\  y-2x < 0\end{array} \right.{(x,y‚àí2x)(‚àíx,‚àí(y‚àí2x))‚Äãwhen y‚àí2x>0when y‚àí2x<0‚Äã

The cost to travel a road between two adjacent villages is 1 pound.
Determine the minimum cost to travel between villages at (x1,y1)(x_1,y_1)(x1‚Äã,y1‚Äã) and (x2,y2)(x_2,y_2)(x2‚Äã,y2‚Äã).

Notes:


The road network is defined so that the villages form an undirected graph.
There are two roads joining (‚àí1,1)(-1,1)(‚àí1,1) and (1,1)(1,1)(1,1), but otherwise there is a single road between any adjacent pair of villages.
The road network does not form a simple grid - that would be too easy!
###Input:


First line will contain TTT, the number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains a single line of input, which has four integers x1,y1,x2,y2x_1, y_1, x_2, y_2x1‚Äã,y1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,y2‚Äã.
###Output:
For each testcase, output in a single line, the answer. That is, the minimum cost in pounds to travel from (x1,y1)(x_1,y_1)(x1‚Äã,y1‚Äã) to (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2‚Äã,y2‚Äã).

###Constraints


T‚â§1000T \le 1000T‚â§1000
The co-ordinates (x1,y1)(x_1,y_1)(x1‚Äã,y1‚Äã) and (x2,y2)(x_2,y_2)(x2‚Äã,y2‚Äã) of the villages satisfy


‚àí1015<x1,x2<1015-10^{15} < x_1,x_2 < 10^{15}‚àí1015<x1‚Äã,x2‚Äã<1015 and
0<y1,y2<10150 < y_1,y_2 < 10^{15}0<y1‚Äã,y2‚Äã<1015
###Subtasks


10 points : ‚àí10<x1,x2<10-10 < x_1,x_2 < 10‚àí10<x1‚Äã,x2‚Äã<10 and 0<y1,y2<100 < y_1,y_2 < 100<y1‚Äã,y2‚Äã<10
10 points : original constraints, but the villages are selected so that the minimum cost for each testcase is <6 < 6<6
30 points: ‚àí106<x1,x2<106-10^6 < x_1,x_2 < 10^6‚àí106<x1‚Äã,x2‚Äã<106 and 0<y1,y2<1060 < y_1,y_2 < 10^60<y1‚Äã,y2‚Äã<106
50 points: original constraints

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, the start and end village co-ordinates are both (7,5)(7,5)(7,5), so the cost is 0.
In the second test case:
From (1,1)(1,1)(1,1) go West to (‚àí1,1)(-1,1)(‚àí1,1) in one step. So the cost is 1.
Note that the South road from (1,1)(1,1)(1,1) also goes to (‚àí1,1)(-1,1)(‚àí1,1).
In the third test case:
From (3,7)(3,7)(3,7) go South to (3,1)(3,1)(3,1).
From (3,1)(3,1)(3,1) go West to (1,1)(1,1)(1,1).
From (1,1)(1,1)(1,1) go West to (‚àí1,1)(-1,1)(‚àí1,1).
From (‚àí1,1)(-1,1)(‚àí1,1) go West to (‚àí3,1)(-3,1)(‚àí3,1).
From (‚àí3,1)(-3,1)(‚àí3,1) go West to (‚àí5,1)(-5,1)(‚àí5,1).
From (‚àí5,1)(-5,1)(‚àí5,1) go North to (5,9)(5,9)(5,9).
